title: The effects of mismatched train and test data cleaning pipelines on regression models: lessons for practice

dataset_schema.label: USCensus
dataset_schema.description: Attributes for individuals including education level, income, race, and age [1, 2].

dataset_schema.label: Airbnb
dataset_schema.description: Property details such as bedrooms/bathrooms, location (latitude/longitude), review scores, and price [1].

original_intent.label: USCensus
original_intent.description: Originally collected as administrative census data to document the United States population [1].

original_intent.label: Airbnb
original_intent.description: Originally generated to provide descriptive information and pricing for property rentals on the Airbnb platform [1].

repurposed_use: These datasets are repurposed to evaluate regression tasks: predicting age for USCensus and predicting listing price for Airbnb [1, 3]. The research investigates how altering data cleaning pipelines between training and testing steps impacts model selection and performance, mimicking real-world scenarios where production data is updated independently of model retraining [4, 5].

limitations.label: Systemic Bias
limitations.description: Minority groups in the USCensus data have a higher frequency of missing values, meaning inconsistent or automated data cleaning pipelines can disproportionately affect these populations and introduce unintended unfairness [2, 6, 7].

limitations.label: Transparency
limitations.description: Adjusting data cleaning pipelines can significantly impact performance, creating a risk that results are presented without standardized comparisons to make model outcomes appear more favorable than they are [8, 9].

limitations.label: Intra-data Inconsistency
limitations.description: The repurposed datasets contain significant internal quality issues, including missing values (up to 20.7% in Airbnb), outliers, and duplicate records that require automated identification and correction [1, 10, 11].

transformations.label: Automated Imputation
transformations.description: Correcting missing values by deleting affected rows or filling gaps with the mean, median, mode, or a 'dummy' placeholder [11, 12].

transformations.label: Outlier Correction
transformations.description: Identifying anomalies using Standard Deviation (SD) or Interquartile Range (IQR) and replacing them with measures of central tendency [11, 13].

transformations.label: Deduplication
transformations.description: Removing duplicate records based on key value columns, such as geographic coordinates in the Airbnb dataset [11, 13].

notes: The study concludes that the method used to clean test data is often more influential on performance than the training cleaning method, and that matching pipelines does not always result in the best performance [14, 15].
